ui.app.title: eSIM Setup Guide
ui.app.subtitle: Pick your device and how you'd like to install — we'll walk you through the rest, step by step.
ui.hero.title: This is easier than it looks
ui.hero.body: Step by step, we'll show you exactly what you'll see on your own phone's screen. Just follow the highlighted buttons — you can't go wrong.
ui.hero.chip3: We show you exactly where to tap
ui.lang.label: Language
ui.delivery.title: How would you like to install it?
ui.delivery.hint: The email we sent you includes everything for all three methods — pick whichever feels easiest. For most people that's the Quick Link.
ui.delivery.link.name: Quick link
ui.delivery.link.desc: One tap on the link in the email.
ui.delivery.link.desc-ios: One tap on the link in the email (works from iOS 17.4).
ui.delivery.link.desc-android: One tap on the link in the email (works from Android 10).
ui.delivery.qr.name: QR code
ui.delivery.qr.desc: Scan the QR code from the email.
ui.delivery.manual.name: Manual details
ui.delivery.manual.desc: Type in the SM-DP+ address and activation code from the email.

steps.ios.before-you-start.title: Before you start
steps.ios.before-you-start.body: Don't worry, this really isn't complicated! eSIM has been available on every iPhone since 2018 (iPhone XS, XS Max, XR or later). One thing worth checking first: your phone needs to be "carrier-unlocked" (not locked to a specific network). Check this under: Settings → General → About → scroll down to "Carrier Lock". If it says "No SIM restrictions", you're good to go! Tip: install your eSIM at home before you travel — you need a stable internet connection at the moment of install.
steps.ios.before-you-start.warning: On iPhones bought in mainland China, eSIM is not available (except iPhone 17e and iPhone Air).
steps.ios.open-link.title: Open the email
steps.ios.open-link.body: Open the Telemeteor email on the iPhone you want to use, then tap the "Apple Quick Link" link.
steps.ios.allow-notification.title: Allow the eSIM to be added
steps.ios.allow-notification.body: An "Activate New eSIM" notification appears at the top of the screen. Tap "Allow".
steps.ios.continue-install.title: Tap Continue
steps.ios.continue-install.body: A confirmation screen appears with the new cellular plan. Tap "Continue".
steps.ios.open-email-qr.title: Open the email and the QR code
steps.ios.open-email-qr.body: Open the Telemeteor email on the iPhone you want to use. Press and hold the QR code image until a menu pops up.
steps.ios.add-esim-prompt.title: Tap "Add eSIM"
steps.ios.add-esim-prompt.body: In the pop-up menu, tap "Add eSIM".
steps.ios.open-camera-qr.title: Open the Camera app
steps.ios.open-camera-qr.body: Open the Camera app and point it at the QR code from the Telemeteor email (on a screen or printed out).
steps.ios.cellular-plan-detected.title: Tap the notification
steps.ios.cellular-plan-detected.body: Once the Camera recognizes the code, a "Cellular Plan Detected" notification appears. Tap it.
steps.ios.continue-qr.title: Tap Continue
steps.ios.add-cellular-plan-qr.title: Tap "Add Cellular Plan"
steps.ios.add-cellular-plan-qr.body: On the final screen, tap "Add Cellular Plan" to start the installation.
steps.ios.open-settings.title: Open Settings
steps.ios.open-settings.body: On your iPhone's home screen, tap the Settings app icon.
steps.ios.open-cellular.title: Go to Cellular
steps.ios.open-cellular.body: In Settings, tap "Cellular" (or "Mobile Data") — the exact name can vary slightly by model.
steps.ios.add-cellular-plan-manual.title: Tap "Add Cellular Plan"
steps.ios.enter-manually.title: Enter the details manually
steps.ios.enter-manually.body: Tap "Enter Details Manually". Carefully paste in the SM-DP+ address and activation code from the email, then fill in the APN (Access Point Name) too, if your phone asks for it.
steps.ios.enter-manually.warning: Enter the details exactly, with no extra spaces — copy-pasting from the email is safest.
steps.ios.confirm-plan.title: Confirm the plan
steps.ios.confirm-plan.body: Review the plan details and tap "Continue". You're almost done!
steps.ios.label-plan.title: Label your plan
steps.ios.label-plan.body: Give the plan a name you'll recognize, e.g. "Travel eSIM" — you can change this later under "Cellular Plan Label" if you skip it now.
steps.ios.line-preferences.title: Keep calls & SMS on your original SIM
steps.ios.line-preferences.body: Under "Default Line", keep your original SIM selected for calls and SMS. This matters because FaceTime, iMessage (iOS only), Viber, WhatsApp and similar apps are tied to your original card's phone number — switching these to the new eSIM would break them. We'll set up the new Telemeteor eSIM separately, just for data, in the next two steps.
steps.ios.enable-data-roaming.title: Turn on data roaming
steps.ios.enable-data-roaming.body: After installing the eSIM, go to: Settings → Mobile Data → Telemeteor → Data Roaming, and turn it ON.
steps.ios.enable-data-roaming.warning: Without this step, your data won't work abroad.
steps.ios.select-data-line.title: On arrival, select Telemeteor
steps.ios.select-data-line.body: When you land in your destination country: Settings → Mobile Data → Mobile Data, and choose the Telemeteor eSIM. From then on this line handles your data, while calls and SMS stay on your original SIM.
steps.ios.select-data-line.warning: Only switch this after you land, so the eSIM's data doesn't run unnecessarily before then.
steps.ios.done.title: Nicely done, you're all set!
steps.ios.done.body: Your Telemeteor eSIM is installed. Don't forget the previous two steps (turning on data roaming and selecting Telemeteor on arrival) — once those are done, you're ready to go!
steps.ios.done.warning: Exact steps and labels can vary slightly by device model and iOS version.
steps.samsung.before-you-start.title: Before you start
steps.samsung.before-you-start.body: Don't worry, this is genuinely simple! eSIM is available on most Galaxy S20 (2020) and later models. The most reliable way to check if yours supports it: open Settings → Connections → SIM card manager — if you see "Add mobile plan" there, you're good to go!
steps.samsung.before-you-start.warning: Some regional variants (e.g. US Galaxy S20) don't have eSIM — the quick check above is what counts.
steps.samsung.open-link.title: Open the email
steps.samsung.open-link.body: Open the Telemeteor email on the phone you want to use, then tap the "Android Quick Link" link.
steps.samsung.add-esim-prefilled.title: Confirm the details
steps.samsung.add-esim-prefilled.body: The "Add eSIM" screen opens automatically with the details already filled in. Tap "Continue".
steps.samsung.open-settings.title: Open Settings
steps.samsung.open-settings.body: On your home screen, tap the Settings app icon.
steps.samsung.open-connections.title: Go to Connections
steps.samsung.open-sim-manager.title: Tap SIM card manager
steps.samsung.add-mobile-plan.title: Tap "Add mobile plan"
steps.samsung.add-using-qr.title: Tap "Add using QR code"
steps.samsung.add-using-qr.body: Choose "Add using QR code", then scan the QR code from the email.
steps.samsung.add-using-code.title: Enter the details manually
steps.samsung.add-using-code.body: Choose "Add using activation code". Carefully paste in the server address (SM-DP+) and activation code from the email, then fill in the APN too, if your phone asks for it.
steps.samsung.add-using-code.warning: Make sure you copy the details exactly, with no extra spaces — copy-pasting straight from the email is safest.
steps.samsung.downloading.title: Almost there!
steps.samsung.downloading.body: Your phone is downloading and activating the plan on your eSIM. This may take a minute.
steps.samsung.enable-data-roaming.title: Turn on data roaming
steps.samsung.enable-data-roaming.body: After installing the eSIM, go to: Settings → Connections → SIM card manager → Telemeteor → Data roaming, and turn it ON.
steps.samsung.enable-data-roaming.warning: Without this step, your data won't work abroad.
steps.samsung.select-data-line.title: On arrival, select Telemeteor
steps.samsung.select-data-line.body: When you land in your destination country: Settings → Connections → SIM card manager, and choose the Telemeteor eSIM for mobile data. Calls and SMS stay on your original SIM.
steps.samsung.select-data-line.warning: Only switch this after you land, so the eSIM's data doesn't run unnecessarily before then.
steps.samsung.done.title: Nicely done, you're all set!
steps.samsung.done.body: Your Telemeteor eSIM is installed. Don't forget the previous two steps (turning on data roaming and selecting Telemeteor on arrival) — once those are done, you're ready to go!
steps.samsung.done.warning: Exact steps and labels can vary slightly by device model and Android version.
steps.pixel.before-you-start.title: Before you start
steps.pixel.before-you-start.body: Don't worry, this is genuinely simple! eSIM is available on every Pixel phone from the Pixel 4 (2019) onward. The most reliable way to check if yours supports it: open Settings → Network & internet → SIMs. (In the US, the Pixel 10 series is eSIM-only and takes no physical SIM.)
steps.pixel.before-you-start.warning: On Pixel 2/3/3a, eSIM support varies by region and carrier.
steps.pixel.open-link.title: Open the email
steps.pixel.open-link.body: Open the Telemeteor email on the phone you want to use, then tap the "Android Quick Link" link.
steps.pixel.add-esim-prefilled.title: Confirm the details
steps.pixel.add-esim-prefilled.body: The eSIM setup screen opens automatically with the details already filled in. Tap "Continue".
steps.pixel.open-settings.title: Open Settings
steps.pixel.open-settings.body: On your home screen, tap the Settings app icon.
steps.pixel.open-network-internet.title: Go to Network & internet
steps.pixel.open-sims.title: Tap SIMs
steps.pixel.add-sim.title: Tap "Add SIM"
steps.pixel.add-sim.body: Tap "Add SIM", then choose "Set up an eSIM".
steps.pixel.scan-qr.title: Scan the QR code
steps.pixel.scan-qr.body: Follow the on-screen instructions and scan the QR code from the email.
steps.pixel.enter-manually.title: Enter the details manually
steps.pixel.enter-manually.body: If you can't scan the QR code, look for "Need help?" or "Enter it manually", then paste in the SM-DP+ address and activation code from the email.
steps.pixel.enter-manually.warning: Wording can vary slightly by model — look for the closest matching option.
steps.pixel.downloading.title: Almost there!
steps.pixel.downloading.body: Your phone is downloading and activating the plan on your eSIM. This may take a minute.
steps.pixel.enable-data-roaming.title: Turn on data roaming
steps.pixel.enable-data-roaming.body: After installing the eSIM, go to: Settings → Network & internet → SIMs → Telemeteor → Data roaming, and turn it ON.
steps.pixel.enable-data-roaming.warning: Without this step, your data won't work abroad.
steps.pixel.select-data-line.title: On arrival, select Telemeteor
steps.pixel.select-data-line.body: When you land in your destination country: Settings → Network & internet → SIMs, and choose the Telemeteor eSIM for mobile data. Calls and SMS stay on your original SIM.
steps.pixel.select-data-line.warning: Only switch this after you land, so the eSIM's data doesn't run unnecessarily before then.
steps.pixel.done.title: Nicely done, you're all set!
steps.pixel.done.body: Your Telemeteor eSIM is installed. Don't forget the previous two steps (turning on data roaming and selecting Telemeteor on arrival) — once those are done, you're ready to go!
steps.pixel.done.warning: Exact steps and labels can vary slightly by device model and Android version.
steps.generic.before-you-start.title: Before you start
steps.generic.before-you-start.body: Since there are so many different Android manufacturers and interfaces (Xiaomi, Huawei, OnePlus, Motorola, Oppo and others), the exact menu names can vary by device. The general recipe: open Settings, and look for "SIM manager", "eSIM" or "Mobile network" — on many phones you can also type "eSIM" into the search bar at the top of Settings.
steps.generic.before-you-start.warning: If you can't find an eSIM setting, your device most likely doesn't support it.
steps.generic.open-link.title: Open the email
steps.generic.open-link.body: Open the Telemeteor email on the phone you want to use, then tap the "Android Quick Link" link. This method needs Android 10+ with up-to-date Google services.
steps.generic.open-link.warning: If nothing happens after tapping, use the QR code or manual method instead.
steps.generic.open-settings.title: Open Settings
steps.generic.open-settings.body: On your home screen, tap the Settings app icon.
steps.generic.find-sim-settings.title: Find the eSIM setting
steps.generic.find-sim-settings.body: In Settings, look for "Network & internet", "Connections" or "SIMs" — or type "eSIM" into the Settings search bar.
steps.generic.find-sim-settings.warning: Menu names differ by manufacturer — look for a similarly named option.
steps.generic.add-esim-qr.title: Scan the QR code
steps.generic.add-esim-qr.body: Look for "Add eSIM" or "Scan QR code", then scan the QR code from the email.
steps.generic.add-esim-manual.title: Enter the details manually
steps.generic.add-esim-manual.body: Look for "Enter manually" or "Use activation code". Carefully paste in the SM-DP+ address and activation code from the email.
steps.generic.add-esim-manual.warning: Make sure you copy the details exactly, with no extra spaces — copy-pasting straight from the email is safest.
steps.generic.downloading.title: Almost there!
steps.generic.downloading.body: Your phone is downloading and activating the plan on your eSIM. This may take a minute.
steps.generic.enable-data-roaming.title: Turn on data roaming
steps.generic.enable-data-roaming.body: After installing the eSIM, find the Telemeteor eSIM row in your SIM settings and turn ON "Data roaming".
steps.generic.enable-data-roaming.warning: Without this step, your data won't work abroad.
steps.generic.select-data-line.title: On arrival, select Telemeteor
steps.generic.select-data-line.body: When you land in your destination country: in your SIM settings, choose the Telemeteor eSIM for mobile data. Calls and SMS stay on your original SIM.
steps.generic.select-data-line.warning: Only switch this after you land, so the eSIM's data doesn't run unnecessarily before then.
steps.generic.done.title: Nicely done, you're all set!
steps.generic.done.body: Your Telemeteor eSIM is installed. Don't forget the previous two steps (turning on data roaming and selecting Telemeteor on arrival) — once those are done, you're ready to go!
steps.generic.done.warning: If you get stuck, your manufacturer's own eSIM guide is the best companion source.
steps.ts.intro.title: eSIM not working?
steps.ts.intro.body: Don't worry! A few simple steps will find the problem. Most issues are fixed in under a minute — just follow the pictures.
steps.ts.esim-on.title: Turn on the Telemeteor eSIM
steps.ts.esim-on.body: Settings → Mobile network (or SIM manager). Tap the Telemeteor eSIM and make sure its switch is turned ON.
steps.ts.region.title: Are you in your destination country?
steps.ts.region.body: A travel eSIM only gives you internet in the country it's for — e.g. a Türkiye plan works in Türkiye. Are you there right now?
steps.ts.wrong-region.title: Then here's the good news 🙂
steps.ts.wrong-region.body: Your eSIM is fine — it will start working once you arrive in your destination. Until then your own SIM stays active. Have a great trip!
steps.ts.roaming.title: Turn on data roaming
steps.ts.roaming.body: Settings → Mobile network → Telemeteor eSIM → Data roaming: turn it ON. This is the most common cause — abroad the eSIM works through roaming.
steps.ts.network.title: Select a network manually
steps.ts.network.body: Settings → Mobile network → Network selection: turn off automatic, wait for the list, and pick an operator. If one doesn't work, try another.
steps.ts.plan.title: Could the plan be expired or out of data?
steps.ts.plan.body: The email you received at purchase shows your plan's validity and data allowance.
steps.ts.topup.title: You'll need a top-up or a new eSIM
steps.ts.topup.body: Write to us and we'll help you top up your plan or choose a new one.
steps.ts.apn.title: Check the APN
steps.ts.apn.body: Settings → Mobile network → Access point names (APN). It must match the one we sent in your email — exactly, with no spaces. If it's different, change it.
steps.ts.success.title: It works! 🎉
steps.ts.success.body: Have a great trip! If anything comes up later, this troubleshooter is always here for you.
steps.ts.support.title: Let's look at it together!
steps.ts.support.body: Tap the button: we'll open a ready-written English email to our team, including everything you've already tried. Just add a short note about what you see and send it — we'll reply quickly.
steps.ts.airplane.title: Turn off airplane mode
steps.ts.airplane.body: Swipe down from the top of the screen to open quick settings, and make sure the airplane ✈️ icon is NOT on (not highlighted).
steps.ts.data-line.title: Use the Telemeteor eSIM for mobile data
steps.ts.data-line.body: In the same place, under "Mobile data", pick the Telemeteor eSIM — not your home SIM. This tells the phone which line to use for internet.
steps.ts.restart.title: Refresh the connection
steps.ts.restart.body: Turn airplane mode on, wait about 30 seconds, then turn it off — or restart your phone. This last step often brings the internet back.
